memory.summary: I have a free afternoon with enough energy to complete my scholarship application, which could significantly reduce next term's costs. While a fun activity is available now, the meaningful financial benefit of the deadline-driven task seems more important.

I've confirmed that starting the scholarship application now creates a safety buffer for unexpected issues, and since the fun activity isn't time-sensitive, I can prioritize this low-stress task today.
I've used half my free block with minimal progress on the scholarship application, leaving little time to finish before the deadline.
I made meaningful progress on the scholarship application but missed the opportunity for a casual game stream, leaving me with less than half the time needed to finish before the deadline.
I've completed 2 hours of the required work, but I still need more time to finish before the deadline with only a small buffer remaining.
I've made steady progress on the scholarship application, but with only 3 hours total needed and a deadline in 3 days, I still need to focus for about 2 more hours.
I've completed over an hour of the required work, but my energy is critically low and I still need to finish the remaining tasks before the deadline.
I advanced the scholarship application slightly, but my critically low energy and sleep debt make continuing now counterproductive.
I advanced the scholarship application slightly, but my critically low energy and sleep debt make continuing now counterproductive.
I've completed 91% of the scholarship application, but my zero energy and high stress make further work counterproductive.
I have nearly finished the scholarship application, but my zero energy and sleep debt mean I must stop now to avoid burnout.
The scholarship application is now complete, so I can stop working on it immediately. My zero energy and high sleep debt confirm that resting is the only productive choice right now.
